text:
Tuesday, December 2, 2025

Byohtadmin|onAugust 15, 2024

Grant Fisher

Grand Blanc grad Grant Fisher, now 27 years old, made history on Saturday Aug. 10 becoming the first American male to earn a medal in both the 5,000 meter and 10,000 meter races in a single Olympics. Fisher captured the bronze in the 10,000 on Aug. 2 in a thrilling finish and on Saturday, lagging behind in sixth place on the final lap, kicked into another gear in the final seconds to pass three runners and clinch the bronze in the 5,000 meter. Fisher posted a 13:15.13.

On Aug. 2, Fisher became just the second American man since 1968 to win a 10,000 meter Olympic Medal, placing third for the bronze medal.

Fisher was notably one of Grand Blanc High’s most successful male runners earning Gatorade Athlete of the Year honors in his senior season. He went on to run for Stanford University and moved to Utah to train for the 2024 Paris Olympics.

In the 2020 Tokyo Olympics, Fisher placed fifth in the 10,000 and ninth in the 5,000.

With the 2028 Summer Games taking place in Los Angeles, Fisher has to be one of the favorites in both races, if he decides to train for another four years.

— Lisa Paine
